52206-guessing of weak passwords, and to sniffing and man-in-the-middle

52211:            <xccdf-1.2:Value id="xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_value_var_accounts_authorized_local_users_regex" type="string" operator="pattern match" interactive="true">
52212-              <xccdf-1.2:title>Accounts Authorized Local Users on the Operating System</xccdf-1.2:title>
52213-              <xccdf-1.2:description>List the user accounts that are authorized locally on the operating system. This list
52214-includes both users required by the operating system and by the installed applications.
52215-Depending on the Operating System distribution, version, software groups and applications,
52216-the user list is different and can be customized with scap-workbench.
--
52260-              <xccdf-1.2:title>Only Authorized Local User Accounts Exist on Operating System</xccdf-1.2:title>
52261-              <xccdf-1.2:description>Enterprise Application tends to use the server or virtual machine exclusively.
52262-Besides the default operating system user, there should be only authorized local
52263-users required by the installed software groups and applications that exist on
52264-the operating system. The authorized user list can be customized in the refine
52265:value variable <html:code>var_accounts_authorized_local_users_regex</html:code>.
52266-OVAL regular expression is used for the user list.
52267-Configure the system so all accounts on the system are assigned to an active system,
52268-application, or user account. Remove accounts that do not support approved system
52269-activities or that allow for a normal user to perform administrative-level actions.
52270-To remove unauthorized system accounts, use the following command:
--
52281-requiring access to the system and application accounts for applications not installed
52282-on the system.</xccdf-1.2:rationale>
